text: I work at Sanofi, and I manage the immunology portfolio.
I am responsible for eight disease areas.

I always review the demand plan on Monday.
My medical reps usually send the reports on Tuesday.
I sometimes travel to other cities.
We never schedule meetings on Friday.
Good morning, Emmanuele. Before August, I want to understand your week. What do you do on Mondays?
On Monday, I always review the demand plan with my team.
Good. And how often do you see the doctors?
I often do a field visit with a medical rep. Usually on Wednesday.
Excellent. And do you travel a lot?
I sometimes travel to other cities. But I never travel on Friday. Friday is for the reports.
And Thursday? We have the global call at three, Paris time. It is very early for you.
Yes, it is early. But I always attend the global call. It is part of the job.
I often do a field visit with a medical rep.
I attend the global call every Thursday.
I sometimes travel, but I never travel on Fridays.
It is part of the job.

Hello Emmanuele. Marc Dubois again, from Paris. I have one small request before August. The Global team wants to understand your week. Not the numbers. The week. How often do you meet your field team? How often do you travel? When do you prepare the reports? Please send me five or six simple sentences. Every week I do this. Every month I do that. Short sentences are perfect. Nobody here expects perfect English. See you soon.

Good morning, everyone, and welcome to the Monday call. My name is Claire Bennett, and this is the agenda for the week. On Monday, we always review the demand plan with each country. On Tuesday, the medical reps send their field reports. On Wednesday, we usually prepare the slides for the global review. On Thursday, every country attends the immunology call at three o'clock, Paris time. And on Friday, we never schedule meetings. Friday is for the reports. If you travel this week, please tell your manager. Thank you, and have a good week.

On Monday, I always review the demand plan with my team. On Tuesday, I usually read the field reports. On Wednesday, I often do a field visit with a medical rep. On Thursday, I always attend the global call. On Friday, I prepare the presentation for the next week.
